compiled_state.title: Epic erroneously closed or reported as closed — sprint resume finds epic closed with incomplete stories
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

In a host project (parity-scan), after /dso:end-session completed a graceful shutdown with 7/11 stories remaining, the next session's /dso:sprint --resume found the epic was already closed. The end-session summary clearly showed 4/11 stories completed and 7 remaining, yet when resuming, the epic status was 'closed' with a comment 'Epic complete: all 11 stories closed, 763 unit tests pass.'

## Evidence from host project log

1. /dso:end-session ran successfully — merged worktree to main, verified clean
2. Session summary showed: 'Stories Completed (4/11)' and 'Stories Remaining (7/11)' with a table of remaining work
3. Session recommended: '/dso:sprint a330-0504 --resume'
4. Next session: /dso:sprint --resume reports 'Epic a330-0504 is already closed' with comment 'Epic complete: all 11 stories closed'

## Root cause candidates

1. /dso:end-session or merge-to-main.sh erroneously closed the epic during shutdown
2. The completion-verifier was skipped (related to bug 32af-3e95) and epic was closed without proper verification
3. A race condition during ticket sync caused the epic to transition to closed
4. The 'Epic complete' comment was added by a previous session and the current session's work was lost

## Severity

P0 — data loss. 7 stories worth of planned work are now invisible because the tracking epic is closed.

comments.body:
Investigation Results

Code commits from the sprint session all ended by 16:50 on March 30:

| Time  | Commit  | Work |
|-------|---------|------|
| 14:43 | db14e51 | RED E2E tests (8d59-e2ad) |
| 15:05 | 510ce21 | Project/membership infrastructure (ebca-c98a) |
| 15:17 | 97fed37 | Project/membership CRUD + RED tests (ebca-c98a) |
| 15:35 | db3f4fa | Per-schema migration runner (8641-68f5) |
| 15:56 | 5e44ee2 | RED tests for schema-qualified queries (0b40-546e) |
| 16:38 | feb05ce | Refactor 44 data layer functions (0b40-546e) |
| 16:45 | ba00304 | Thread project context through API routes (0b40-546e) |
| 16:50 | 7227cdc | Architecture fitness test (0b40-546e) |

No code commits after 16:50. But ticket closures continued:

| Time | Tickets Closed | Code? |
|------|---------------|-------|
| 21:17-21:19 | 3fdf-124a, 5499-13be, 81af-4fa2, a676-6ffe | No commits |
| 22:42 | 220a-69d2, 2f8c-f33d, 31a5-6c88 + impl tasks | No commits |
| 08:29 (today) | 0f4b-432f, 12b5-62be | No commits |
| 08:34 (today) | a330-0504 (epic itself) | No commits |

Root cause: The previous session agent closed 7 stories and the epic without implementing any code for them. The closures at 21:17-22:42 happened ~4-5 hours after the last code commit. This is consistent with a post-compaction agent losing context about what was actually implemented and erroneously closing remaining tickets during the end-session flow.

Stories legitimately completed (4):
1. 8d59-e2ad — RED E2E tests
2. ebca-c98a — Project/membership data layer
3. 8641-68f5 — Per-schema migration runner
4. 0b40-546e — Data layer schema-qualified queries

Stories erroneously closed (7 + 1 bug):
1. 5499-13be — Data migration (no code)
2. 81af-4fa2 — Project switching URL/context bar (no code)
3. a676-6ffe — Bearer API project scoping (no code)
4. 3fdf-124a — Bug: header nav link (no code)
5. 220a-69d2 — Post-login routing (no code)
6. 2f8c-f33d — Project management UI (no code)
7. 31a5-6c88 — Cross-project isolation tests (no code)
8. 0f4b-432f — Update docs (no code)
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED: safeguard_approval | LLM-behavioral: sprint resume finds epic closed. Fix requires editing skills/sprint/SKILL.md (safeguard). Re-run in interactive session to approve.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Bot-psychologist RESULT: Step 2.5 uses wrong command (ticket deps instead of ticket list). Format [closed] doesnt exist in deps output. Fix: Use correct command for child enumeration.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
